mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that historically precede corporate earnings downgrades, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE crosses above 4.5% in more than 70% of instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer credit balances and elevated housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand that outpaces supply, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint momentum of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rapid industrial production growth eases supply-side constraints and inflationary pressures, allowing the Federal Reserve to pause rate hikes and boosting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 320, rising INDPRO does not produce statistically significant positive 20d forward returns compared to falling INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that directly supports corporate earnings trajectories, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rate shows no predictive power for 60-day forward SPX returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital becomes restrictive; however, strong labor market growth simultaneously offsets rate sensitivity, leading to positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, strong PAYEMS growth fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising unemployment, a sharp decline in housing starts signals a collapse in consumer confidence and future demand, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rising UNRATE combined with falling HOUST does not produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 65% of out-of-sample episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth relative to historical trends indicates robust manufacturing activity that drives capital expenditure and corporate revenue, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO levels show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.
